skill.name: create-skill-file
skill.description: Guides AI agents through creating clear SKILL.md files with strong names, activation descriptions, examples, templates, and quality checks.
skill.summary: Create structured SKILL.md files for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
skill.author: labring

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network, credential, crypto, and obfuscation patterns. Review found these are markdown examples, templates, placeholder variable names, and documentation links, with no executable scripts, automatic network calls, prompt injection, or secret exfiltration. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because users should still review command examples before copying them into new skills.

low_findings.title: Documentation-only command examples
low_findings.description: Static command detections are markdown code examples and templates for writing skills. They are not executable package code and no automatic execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched shell commands appear inside markdown examples that teach skill authors how to document workflows. No script file, install hook, or hidden execution path was present in the reviewed file set.

low_findings.title: Placeholder credential and environment names
low_findings.description: Environment and secret detections are placeholder names used in examples, such as INTERNAL_API_KEY, SECRET_KEY, and APP_DATABASE_URL. No real secret value or outbound use of a secret was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The strings are generic placeholders inside instructional content. I found no evidence of credential harvesting, persistence, or transmission.

low_findings.title: Reference and example URLs
low_findings.description: Network detections are documentation links, localhost URLs, or hypothetical service URLs in deployment examples. No code automatically calls these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references or illustrative endpoints. There is no runtime fetch logic or evidence of data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and obfuscation heuristics dismissed
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and entropy alerts appear to be triggered by markdown terms such as SKILL.md and repeated description fields. No MD5, SHA-1, packed binary content, or encrypted payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found normal markdown and bilingual documentation, not cryptographic implementation or obfuscated executable content. The high-entropy alert is consistent with dense documentation text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Operational shell commands in examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains deployment, migration, test, and rollback command examples. They are documentation-only, but users should adapt and review them before placing similar commands in active skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are real shell patterns, including production-style deployment commands. The low risk rating reflects that they are examples and are not executed by the skill itself.

content.value_statement: Skill authors often create vague or oversized SKILL.md files that are hard for agents to activate. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical rules, templates, examples, and checklists for better skill files.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md frontmatter fields and naming rules.
Shows how to write activation-focused descriptions with clear trigger keywords.
Provides progressive disclosure guidance for splitting large skills into references and examples.
Includes basic and workflow skill templates that authors can adapt.
Compares good and bad skill examples with concrete improvement notes.
Offers a quality checklist for structure, examples, validation, and maintainability.
limitations: It does not automatically create or validate files in a repository.
Most guidance text is written in Chinese, so teams may need translation.
The examples are generic and must be adapted before production use.
It does not replace security review for skills that run scripts or access services.

use_cases.title: Create a First Agent Skill
use_cases.description: Use the basic structure, naming rules, and examples to draft a complete SKILL.md file.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Authoring
use_cases.description: Apply shared templates and quality checks so a team writes consistent, discoverable skills.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Skill Documentation
use_cases.description: Compare an existing skill against the good and bad examples to remove vague content and add clear triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-skill-file guidance to draft a SKILL.md for a skill that helps with [task]. Include name, description, when to use, workflow, examples, and a checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Activation Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill name and description: [paste text]. Make the name specific, write a third-person description, and add trigger keywords users would actually say.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this repeated workflow into a workflow skill: [describe workflow]. Include phases, validation points, error handling, examples, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this SKILL.md against the create-skill-file checklist: [paste content]. Identify unclear triggers, overlong sections, missing examples, unsafe commands, and improvements.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for database migration work.
output_examples.output: A structured skill draft with a specific name, activation description, migration workflow, safety checks, examples, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my existing skill description and make it easier to activate.
output_examples.output: A revised description with concrete trigger phrases, target technologies, and clear boundaries for when the skill should be used.

output_examples.input: Convert our release checklist into an agent skill.
output_examples.output: A workflow-oriented skill outline with preparation checks, ordered release steps, rollback guidance, and a final quality checklist.

best_practices: Use specific names and descriptions that include realistic trigger keywords.
Keep SKILL.md focused on agent-specific guidance, not general tutorials.
Add examples, validation steps, and troubleshooting notes for repeatable workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague names such as helper, utility, or manager.
Do not include broad programming tutorials that the model already knows.
Do not add operational commands without prerequisites, validation, and rollback guidance.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create SKILL.md files with clear metadata, activation rules, workflows, examples, and checks.

faq.question: Is it only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code style agent workflows.

faq.question: Does it generate files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions, examples, and templates that an agent or author can apply.

faq.question: Can teams use it for shared standards?
faq.answer: Yes. Teams can use the templates and checklist to keep skill files consistent.

faq.question: Why are there good and bad examples?
faq.answer: The examples show common mistakes and demonstrate how to make skills more specific and useful.

faq.question: Should command examples be copied directly?
faq.answer: No. Review commands, paths, and environment settings before using them in a real project.
